headline: AI trade could power higher in second half - Wells Fargo summer rally call
detail: Two bullish macro signals converging: Wells Fargo explicitly telling clients to position for a summer rally, and a separate analysis suggesting AI momentum could accelerate with not much more fuel needed. For your book - which is ~90% tech/chip - this is the most directly relevant macro story today. CRWD, SMH, SOXL, QQQ, TECL all benefit if this plays out.
rec: CRWD is the cleanest play here - you already own it, it's green, scanner backs an add at $741-742 with stop $740.31. Keep size small given the tight stop. SOXL would be the levered bet on chips but given the -7% hole you're already in, adding leverage here on margin needs serious caution.

headline: White House AI crackdown opens door for Chinese model makers
detail: Any policy friction around US AI development is a headwind for the AI-infrastructure names that underpin your holdings - chips, cloud security, semiconductor equipment. If Chinese competitors close the gap, pricing power and growth narratives for US names get questioned. This is a slow-burn risk, not a today sell signal, but worth tracking if it escalates.
rec: No immediate trade - this doesn't warrant selling your positions today. But if this story gains traction, your most exposed names are SMH and SOXL. Keep it on the radar.

headline: Small-cap stocks best first half in 35 years
detail: Rotation into small caps means money has been moving away from mega-cap tech, at least partially. Your book is overwhelmingly large-cap tech and leveraged tech ETFs, so sustained small-cap outperformance could mean relative underperformance and slower recovery for names like SMH and SOXL. Not a crisis, but context for why your chip names may feel sluggish.
rec: No trade triggered by this directly. Just context - if small-cap rotation continues, don't be surprised if your tech names lag the headline index numbers.

headline: Trump Iran strikes and 2026 pattern data
detail: Geopolitical strikes historically create short-term volatility spikes then quick reversals in equities. For leveraged positions like SOXL and margin-amplified holdings, even a brief volatility spike can trigger stops or force uncomfortable drawdowns before any recovery. Worth being aware of given your leverage exposure.
rec: No action needed now, but this is exactly the kind of overnight or pre-market shock that hits leveraged ETFs hardest. Make sure your SOXL $200 stop is actually set as a real order, not just a mental note.
